perturbation.original_text: The obligation not to disclose Confidential Information shall remain in effect until 2 years from the date hereof or until the Confidential Information ceases to be a trade secret, except to the extent that such Confidential Information is excluded from the obligations of confidentiality under this Agreement pursuant to Paragraph 2 above.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligation not to disclose Confidential Information shall remain in effect until 1 year from the date hereof or until the Confidential Information ceases to be a trade secret, except to the extent that such Confidential Information is excluded from the obligations of confidentiality under this Agreement pursuant to Paragraph 2 above.
perturbation.explanation: The modification of the confidentiality period from 2 years to 1 year creates an in-text contradiction with section 9, where it mentions that 'This Agreement will terminate on the earlier of: (c) 5 years from the date hereof.' . This creates uncertainty as the agreement now has conflicting terms regarding the duration of confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement will terminate on the earlier of: (a) the written agreement of the parties to terminate this Agreement; (b) the consummation of the Transaction; or (c) 5 years from the date hereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the obligation not to disclose confidential information remains in effect until 2 years. This was changed to 1 year. The contradicted text states that the agreement terminates in 5 years. This creates a contradiction because the confidentiality clause now expires potentially before the agreement itself terminates. The obligation of non-disclosure is directly contradicted by the termination clause, satisfying the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction and creating a direct conflict in duration.

perturbation.original_text: Upon the termination of this Agreement, the Receiving Party will ensure that all documents, memoranda, notes and other writings or electronic records prepared by it that include or reflect any Confidential Information are returned or destroyed as directed by the Disclosing Party
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the termination of this Agreement, the Receiving Party is only required to return documents specifically marked as confidential. Other documents may be retained for archival purposes.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction. The original text requires all documents containing confidential information to be returned or destroyed. The modified text allows the receiving party to retain some documents for archival purposes, contradicting the initial requirement for complete return/destruction. This creates ambiguity about what must be returned and what can be kept, impacting Section 3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Obligation to Maintain Confidentiality. With respect to Confidential Information: a. The Receiving Party and its Representatives agree to retain the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party in strict confidence, to protect the security, integrity and confidentiality of such information and to not permit unauthorized access to or unauthorized use, disclosure, publication or dissemination of Confidential Information except in conformity with this Agreement; b. The Receiving Party and its Representatives shall adopt and/or maintain security processes and procedures to safeguard the confidentiality of all Confidential Information received by the Disclosing Party using a reasonable degree of care, but not less than that degree of care used in safeguarding its own similar information or material; c. Upon the termination of this Agreement, the Receiving Party is only required to return documents specifically marked as confidential. Other documents may be retained for archival purposes.; d. If there is an unauthorized disclosure or loss of any of the Confidential Information by the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives, the Receiving Party will promptly, at its own expense, notify the Disclosing Party in writing and take all actions as may be necessary or reasonably requested by the Disclosing Party to minimize any damage to the Disclosing Party or a third party as a result of the disclosure or loss; and e. The obligation not to disclose Confidential Information shall remain in effect until 2 years from the date hereof or until the Confidential Information ceases to be a trade secret, except to the extent that such Confidential Information is excluded from the obligations of confidentiality under this Agreement pursuant to Paragraph 2 above.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated the return or destruction of ALL documents containing Confidential Information upon termination. The changed text introduces an exception, allowing the Receiving Party to retain certain documents for archival purposes. This directly contradicts the 'Obligation to Maintain Confidentiality' clause, specifically part (a) which requires the Receiving Party to retain Confidential Information in strict confidence and (c), which introduces the same contradictory language that was added to the original text. Because the modified text provides conflicting obligations regarding the handling of confidential information, and since it goes directly against the other cited section, this causes a direct contradiction that would cause confusion and potentially unenforceability, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party will take reasonable steps to ensure that its Representatives adhere to the terms of this Agreement. The Receiving Party will be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party will take reasonable steps to ensure that its Representatives adhere to the terms of this Agreement. The Receiving Party will only be responsible for breaches of this Agreement by its direct employees, not by independent contractors acting as Representatives.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by limiting the Receiving Party's responsibility for breaches caused by Representatives to only direct employees, while excluding independent contractors. This contradicts the original statement that the Receiving Party is responsible for any breach by *any* of its Representatives. This creates uncertainty about who bears the responsibility for a breach, especially in a situation where an independent contractor violates the NDA, contradicting section 5.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Representatives. The Receiving Party will take reasonable steps to ensure that its Representatives adhere to the terms of this Agreement. The Receiving Party will only be responsible for breaches of this Agreement by its direct employees, not by independent contractors acting as Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The changed text limits the Receiving Party's responsibility to only direct employees, excluding independent contractors. This directly contradicts the original statement, which held the Receiving Party responsible for breaches by *any* of its Representatives. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, but the change is what creates the contradiction. The modification introduces a clear inconsistency in who is responsible for breaches of the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees that use or disclosure of any Confidential Information in a manner inconsistent with this Agreement will give rise to irreparable injury for which: (a) money damages may not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by such party; (b) the other party may be entitled to specific performance and injunction and other equitable relief with respect to any such breach; (c) such remedies will not be the exclusive remedies for any such breach, but will be in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity; and (d) in the event of litigation relating to this Agreement, if a court of competent jurisdiction determines in a final non-appealable order that one party, or any of its Representatives, has breached this Agreement, such party will be liable for reasonable legal fees and expenses incurred by the other party in connection with such litigation, including, but not limited to, any appeals.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees that use or disclosure of any Confidential Information in a manner inconsistent with this Agreement will give rise to irreparable injury for which: (a) money damages may be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by such party; (b) the other party may be entitled to specific performance; (c) such remedies will be the exclusive remedies for any such breach and (d) in the event of litigation relating to this Agreement, if a court of competent jurisdiction determines in a final non-appealable order that one party, or any of its Representatives, has breached this Agreement, such party will NOT be liable for reasonable legal fees and expenses incurred by the other party in connection with such litigation, including, but not limited to, any appeals.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text now states that 'money damages may be a sufficient remedy,' contradicting the original statement. It also limits the remedies to being exclusive and removes the liability for legal fees in case of a breach. These alterations directly contradict the original intent of Section 7, creating uncertainty about the available remedies and financial responsibilities in case of a breach of contract, by making the legal recourse limited.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Remedies. Each party agrees that use or disclosure of any Confidential Information in a manner inconsistent with this Agreement will give rise to irreparable injury for which: (a) money damages may be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by such party; (b) the other party may be entitled to specific performance; (c) such remedies will be the exclusive remedies for any such breach and (d) in the event of litigation relating to this Agreement, if a court of competent jurisdiction determines in a final non-appealable order that one party, or any of its Representatives, has breached this Agreement, such party will NOT be liable for reasonable legal fees and expenses incurred by the other party in connection with such litigation, including, but not limited to, any appeals.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the 'Remedies' section. The original text states that money damages may *not* be a sufficient remedy, remedies are *not* exclusive, and the breaching party *will* be liable for legal fees. The changed text contradicts this by stating that money damages *may* be a sufficient remedy, remedies *will* be exclusive, and the breaching party *will not* be liable for legal fees. This creates a direct conflict within the contract regarding available remedies and liability for legal fees, thus introducing a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement will be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Minnesota, without regard to the principles of conflict of laws. Each party consents to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts located in the State of Minnesota for any legal action, suit or proceeding arising out of or in connection with this Agreement. Each party further waives any objection to the laying of venue for any such suit, action or proceeding in such courts.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement will be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware, without regard to the principles of conflict of laws. Each party consents to the non-exclusive jurisdiction of the courts located in the State of Minnesota for any legal action, suit or proceeding arising out of or in connection with this Agreement. Each party reserves the right to object to the laying of venue for any such suit, action or proceeding in such courts.
perturbation.explanation: The change in governing law from Minnesota to Delaware creates a contradiction, as the agreement previously specified Minnesota law. Changing 'exclusive' to 'non-exclusive' and removing the waiver of venue objection further contradicts the original clause. This creates uncertainty over which state's laws apply and where legal proceedings should take place, impacting the enforceability of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Jurisdiction. This Agreement will be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware, without regard to the principles of conflict of laws. Each party consents to the non-exclusive jurisdiction of the courts located in the State of Minnesota for any legal action, suit or proceeding arising out of or in connection with this Agreement. Each party reserves the right to object to the laying of venue for any such suit, action or proceeding in such courts.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the governing law from Minnesota to Delaware and changes the jurisdiction from exclusive to non-exclusive, while also reserving the right to object to venue. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which has the same clauses as the Changed text, creating a strong inconsistency and uncertainty about which state's laws apply and where legal proceedings should take place. This contradicts the original clause where Minnesota law was chosen.
